core_contribution: This paper provides a comprehensive taxonomy and review of multiple kernel learning (MKL) algorithms that combine multiple kernels instead of using a single kernel in SVMs. The authors categorize existing MKL methods along six dimensions: learning method, functional form, target function, training method, base learner, and computational complexity.
algorithm_steps: Step 1: Define multiple kernels {k_m} corresponding to different feature representations or similarity notions
Step 2: Choose combination function f_η to combine kernels (linear, nonlinear, or data-dependent)
Step 3: Learn combination parameters η using one of five learning methods (fixed rules, heuristics, optimization, Bayesian, or boosting)
Step 4: Train base learner (typically SVM) using combined kernel k_η(x_i, x_j) = f_η({k_m(x_i^m, x_j^m)})
Step 5: Use either one-step (simultaneous/sequential) or two-step (iterative) training methodology
key_equations: Linear combination: k_η(x_i,x_j) = Σ_{m=1}^P η_m k_m(x_i^m,x_j^m)
Feature space concatenation: Φ_η(x) = [√η_1 Φ_1(x_1), √η_2 Φ_2(x_2), ..., √η_P Φ_P(x_P)]^T

key_assumptions.assumption: Multiple kernels provide complementary information that can be beneficially combined
key_assumptions.why_needed: The fundamental premise of MKL is that combining kernels yields better performance than any single kernel
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When one kernel is clearly superior and others add only noise or redundant information

key_assumptions.assumption: Kernel combination parameters can be learned effectively from training data
key_assumptions.why_needed: MKL methods must be able to determine optimal weights/parameters for kernel combination
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When training data is insufficient to reliably estimate combination parameters or when the optimal combination is highly data-dependent

key_assumptions.assumption: The chosen functional form for combination (linear, nonlinear, data-dependent) is appropriate for the problem
key_assumptions.why_needed: Different combination strategies work better for different types of kernels and problems
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When using linear combination for complex interactions that require nonlinear combination, or vice versa

baseline_limitations_identified: Single kernel methods may be suboptimal due to kernel selection bias and inability to leverage multiple information sources or similarity notions simultaneously
proposed_improvement: MKL methods automatically learn to combine multiple kernels, avoiding manual kernel selection and potentially leveraging complementary information from different kernels
condition_where_baseline_wins: When one kernel is clearly optimal for the problem and additional kernels only introduce noise, or when training data is too limited to reliably learn combination parameters
suitable_toy_datasets: Two-moon dataset with multiple feature representations
Iris dataset with different kernel types
Synthetic classification data with multiple views
toy_dataset_justification: These datasets allow testing different kernel combinations and are simple enough to verify that MKL methods can learn appropriate kernel weights
reproducibility_notes: This is primarily a survey paper, but implementing the reviewed MKL algorithms would require understanding multiple optimization frameworks (SDP, QCQP, SOCP) and various base learners

key_components_for_ablation.component: Kernel weight constraints (conic vs convex vs linear)
key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Determines the feasible space for kernel combination parameters
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Unconstrained weights may lead to overfitting or loss of interpretability

key_components_for_ablation.component: Regularization on kernel weights (ℓ1, ℓ2, mixed norms)
key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Controls sparsity and complexity of kernel combination
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: May result in dense, non-sparse solutions without automatic kernel selection

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Determines how combination parameters and base learner parameters are optimized
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Different optimization strategies may converge to different local optima

known_failure_modes.scenario: When kernels are highly correlated or redundant
known_failure_modes.reason: Combination parameters become difficult to estimate reliably

known_failure_modes.scenario: With limited training data relative to number of kernels
known_failure_modes.reason: Insufficient data to learn reliable combination parameters
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Kernel combination parameters can be learned effectively from training data

known_failure_modes.scenario: When computational complexity of optimization becomes prohibitive
known_failure_modes.reason: SDP and SOCP problems scale poorly compared to standard QP
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: The optimization problem for learning combination parameters is tractable
